revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain-tile sheet for game development. Profile snes16_platformer_v1, preset village_path_transition_16. The image content must be an exact 4 row x 4 column tileset, 16 frames total, each tile exactly 16x16 pixels, filling the canvas edge to edge with no gutters, no borders, no visible grid lines, no tile IDs, no labels, no UI, no watermark.

Subject: top-down packed light-earth footpath transition through deep green mythical-Japan satoyama grass. Bright cheerful early-summer palette: deep rich saturated rice-field-bank greens, moist brown soil, clear blue-green water accents only as tiny natural hints if useful, vermilion and white accent pixels only as tiny natural decoration marks. Soft warm sunlight from top-left. SFC/SNES 16-bit style, crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, readable pixel clusters, consistent palette, subtle one-pixel dark outline where useful.

Strict layout:
Row 1: four full CENTER path variations, cohesive packed light-earth soil, faint stepping-stone and pebble hints, varied texture.
Row 2: edge transition tiles with neighboring grass terrain on the North side, South side, West side, East side, in that order. Organic broken grass overlap, no straight rectangular bands.
Row 3: outer corner transition tiles NW, NE, SW, SE, in that order. Soft irregular grass-to-path corners, tilemap-compatible.
Row 4: four accent center variations: chipped edge center, grass-overlap center, small pebble/stepping-stone center, subtle satoyama accent center. Keep accents tiny and terrain-only.

Tilemap quality: every cell completely filled edge to edge and seamlessly tileable. Avoid repeated square stamps and checkerboard read; vary edge breakup, soil texture, grass clusters, lighting, and small decorations while keeping identity and scale consistent. Transitions must blend naturally, not form hard rectangular bands. Use moist light-earth path and deep green grass; not dry, not yellow-brown, not western prairie, not wasteland.

Containment: every frame stays inside its own 16x16 cell and keeps the same scale. No gaps between cells. If any truly empty canvas space would remain, fill it with solid #FF00FF magenta, but do not put magenta inside usable terrain pixels.

Avoid all characters, animals, companion creatures, franchise logos or likenesses, samurai armor, helmets, ninja outfits, swords, guns, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, letter emblems, text, watermarks, UI elements, and any protected-IP recognizable content.
